sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text:
This standard applies to mozzarella intended for direct consumption or for further processing, in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

The principal starter culture microorganisms are Streptococcus thermophilus and/or Lactococcus spp. Products made from buffalo milk shall be salted in cold brine.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Mozzarella is an unripened cheese in conformity with the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978)2 and the Standard for Unripened Cheese Including Fresh Cheese  (CXS 221-2001).3 It is a smooth, elastic cheese with a long stranded parallel-orientated fibrous protein structure without evidence of curd granules. The cheese is rindlessi and may be formed into various shapes. Mozzarella with a high moisture content is a soft cheese with overlying layers that may form pockets containing liquid of milky appearance. It may be packed with or without the liquid. The cheese has a near white colour. Mozzarella with a low moisture content is a firm/semi-hard homogeneous cheese without holes and is suitable for shredding. Mozzarella is made by “pasta filata” processing, which consists of heating curd of a suitable pH value kneading and stretching until the curd is smooth and free from lumps. Still warm, the curd is cut and moulded, then firmed by cooling. Other  processing techniques, which give end products with the same physical, chemical and organoleptic characteristics are allowed.

sections.title: Permitted ingredients
sections.text: – starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/or flavour producing bacteria and cultures of other harmless microorganisms; – rennet or other safe and suitable coagulating enzymes; – sodium chloride and potassium chloride as a salt substitute; – safe and suitable processing aids; – vinegar; – potable water; and – rice, corn and potato flours and starches: Notwithstanding the provisions in the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978),3 these substances can be used in the same function as anti-caking agents for treatment of the surface of cut, sliced, and shredded mozzarella with a low moisture content only, provided they are added only in amounts functionally necessary as governed by good manufacturing practice, taking into account any use of the anti-caking agents listed in Section 4.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The milk used in the manufacture of the products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: Name of the food
sections.text: The name mozzarella may be applied in accordance with Section 4.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), provided that the product is in conformity with this standard. Where customary in the country of retail sale, alternative spelling may be used. The use of the name is an option that may be chosen only if the cheese complies with this standard. Where the name is not used for a cheese that complies with this standard, the naming provisions of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978) apply. The designation of mozzarella with a high moisture content shall be accompanied by a qualifying term describing the true nature of the product. The designation of products in which the fat content is below or above the reference range but above the absolute minimum specified in Section 3.3 of this standard shall be accompanied by an appropriate qualification describing the modification made or the fat content (expressed as fat in dry matter or as percentage by mass whichever is acceptable in the country of retail sale), either as part of the name or in a prominent position in the same field of vision. Suitable qualifiers are the appropriate characterizing terms specified in Section 7.3 of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978) or a nutritional claim in accordance with the Guidelines for the Use of Nutritional Claims (CXG 23-1997). The designation may also be used for cut, sliced, shredded, or grated products made from cheese which cheese is in conformity with this standard.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR MOZZARELLA
pages.summary: Página apresenta título do documento como título principal e um cabeçalho padrão. Contém texto indicativo sobre adoção e emendas. Estrutura simples sem seções subsequentes.

pages.title: CXS 262-2006
pages.summary: Página contém dois nós principais sem numeração, identificados como seções de emendas para os anos 2022 e 2023, cada uma com seu texto explicativo direto. Document title e header foram extraídos.

Página contém três seções principais numeradas (1, 2, 3) com seus respectivos textos. A seção 3 possui duas subseções (3.1 e 3.2) com seus conteúdos. Todos os títulos aparecem na página e o texto está corretamente associado. Não há continuidade entre páginas para estes nós.
Página contém seção 3.3 Composition com texto detalhado de composição do leite incluindo tabelas e notas. A seção é continuação da anterior e continua na próxima página.

A página contém as seções 4, 5 e 6 do documento. A seção 4, FOOD ADDITIVES, continua da página anterior e segue para a próxima página porque seu conteúdo e tabela se estendem. As seções 5 e 6 são iniciadas e concluídas nesta página sem continuação.
A página contém as seções 7 e 8 do documento. A seção 7 'LABELLING' possui quatro subseções (7.1 a 7.4). O conteúdo está completo nesta página, sem indicações de continuidade para outras páginas.

A página contém 2 seções principais: 'APPENDIX – ADDITIONAL INFORMATION' e 'NOTES'. Dentro do apêndice, a subseção '1 Method of manufacture' está presente com seus dois parágrafos explicativos. O conteúdo está completamente contido nesta página, sem indicações de continuidade para outras páginas.
